definitions: To provide a class or component with an instance of a repository dependency, typically through a constructor, method, or property, rather than having the class create it itself.
To supply a software module with a data access layer or storage mechanism, often for testing or to adhere to the dependency inversion principle.
examples: We need to inject repository into the service layer.
The framework will automatically inject repository for us.
Developers often inject repository to facilitate unit testing.
